meta.release_notes:
# Changelog

All notable changes to this model will be documented in this file.

## [Purpose of this model]

The "Chemical Material Passport" data model implements a digital product passport for chemical and intermediate products.  It addresses the description of raw materials and (chemical) products, the starting point for essential automotive components and the processing of the like. The Chemical Material Passport is intended to summarize all relevant information as required by regulation, in particular the EU Ecodesign Regulation (ESPR) but also enabling partners in the value chain to exchange information about this material in digital formats, including safety data sheets, certificates of analysis and other data.

## [3.0.0] - 2025-03-21

### Changed
- Certificate of Analysis (CoA), Technical Information & Safety Data Sheets (SDS) can be included in Chemical Material Passport in structured or (less favored for legacy in) binary formats.

### Removed

## [2.0.0] - 2024-05-21

### Changed

- connection update to @prefix ext-passport: <urn:samm:io.catenax.generic.digital_product_passport:5.0.0#>
- changed safetyDocument to safetyDocumentation and changed payload name
- moved methodName together with "condition" and "description" of the method
- changed payload name from unspecific to generic on highest level

## [1.0.0] - 2024-04-22

### Added

- initial model

### Changed

n/a

### Removed

nodes.label: ChemicalMaterialPassport
nodes.description: The information in a Digital Product Passport allows stakeholders in the value chain to map information and obtain a better understanding of the composition of the product, the environmental impact of the production and use phase, and the aspect of circularity. To serve the same purpose as the Digital Passport, the Chemical Material Passport also plays an important role in providing more detailed information in terms of sustainability, safety and waste management. It can replace the current way of communication and data exchange from multi-channels by one streamlined data flow.

nodes.label: productSpecificParameters
nodes.description: Product specific parameters of the electric drive.

nodes.label: productUnspecificParameters
nodes.description: The product unspecific parameters. These are defined by the Digital Product Passport proposal from the ESPR. The main basis is provided by the document "Proposal for a REGULATION OF THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT AND OF THE COUNCIL establishing a framework for setting ecodesign requirements for sustainable products and repealing Directive 2009/125/EC" from March 30th, 2022. The latest version of the document was the provisional agreement between the EU Council and the Parliament from January 9th, 2024.

nodes.label: parameter
nodes.description: Parameters of the chemical material.

nodes.label: productType
hazardAssessment
nodes.description: Hazard assessment refers to evaluating and identifying potential risks or dangers associated with the specific material.

nodes.label: transport
nodes.description: Transport related information.

nodes.label: disposal
nodes.description: The safe and appropriate elimination or management of these materials once they are no longer needed or usable.

nodes.label: safety
nodes.description: Safety related attributes of the chemical material.

nodes.label: compliance
nodes.description: Compliance refers to adhering to relevant regulations, standards, or specifications regarding the testing, documentation, and presentation of parameters, methods, and results. This ensures that the product meets required safety, quality, and regulatory standards throughout its lifecycle, from manufacturing to post-application use.

nodes.label: parameterBeforeUse
nodes.description: Parameters of the chemical material before use.

nodes.label: parameterAfterUse
nodes.description: Parameters of the chemical material after use.

nodes.label: classification
nodes.description: Classification for the hazard assessment.

nodes.label: labeling
nodes.description: The material labeling.

nodes.label: hazardAssessmentDocumentation
nodes.description: Documentation providing hazard assessment information for ensuring the safe management of chemical materials throughout their lifecycle.

nodes.label: productTransport
nodes.description: Transport related attributes, which are not UN related.

nodes.label: unTransport
nodes.description: Transport related attributes from the UN.

nodes.label: disposalBeforeUse
nodes.description: Management and disposal practices for chemicals that are no longer needed or deemed unsuitable for their intended purpose. This information can be provided as a document, detailing the properties of waste that render it hazardous, along with disposal and recovery operations or recommendations, and accidental release measures.

nodes.label: disposalAfterUse
nodes.description: Chemical Management and Disposal Guides post-usage as document.

nodes.label: disposalWasteCode
nodes.description: A waste code is a standardized alphanumeric code used to classify different types of waste according to their characteristics, origin, and potential hazards. These codes help in proper identification, handling, treatment, and disposal of waste materials.

nodes.label: disposalPackagingInformation
nodes.description: Guidelines and instructions regarding the proper management and disposal of packaging materials after use. Includes instructions on separation, reuse, and recycling.

nodes.label: safetyDocument
nodes.description: Safety measure information in a document that outlines safety protocols, procedures, and guidelines to be followed in specific situations involving the product. It provides clear instructions on accident prevention, how to respond to emergencies, and mitigate potential hazards. Examples include exposure scenarios, safety data sheets, risk assessments, and information on safety equipment and personal protective equipment (PPE).

nodes.label: emergencyPhone
nodes.description: A phone number in case of emergency serves the vital purpose of providing immediate access to emergency responders.

nodes.label: firstAidDocument
nodes.description: Information on how to proceed in case of an emergency is essential. It includes guidance on providing initial assistance or immediate care to a person who has been injured before professional medical help arrives.

nodes.label: complianceCountry
nodes.description: The name of the country of compliance denotes the country whose rules and regulations the product must adhere to.

nodes.label: complianceRegulationName
complianceResult
nodes.description: The statement indicates whether the product complies with the aforementioned regulations.

nodes.label: complianceReasonForExemption
nodes.description: Data providing details and justifications for an exemption from compliance with certain regulations, standards, or requirements. This information assists regulatory authorities or stakeholders in evaluating the merits of the exemption and making decisions in accordance with legal and regulatory requirements.

nodes.label: complianceRemark
nodes.description: If necessary, any remarks that provide relevant information for compliance. Further clarification supplements the compliance information, offering additional context or details to ensure thorough understanding and adherence to regulatory requirements.

nodes.label: productParameterName
nodes.description: The name of the parameter for the product tested and presented, for instance, in the Certificate of Analysis or Safety Data Sheet, found in Section 9. Parameters for chemical products usually encompass their chemical composition, physical properties, performance characteristics, and toxicological aspects. These parameters vary depending on the chemical's nature, its intended use, and relevant safety or regulatory considerations.

nodes.label: productParameterValue
nodes.description: The value resulting from the tested parameter is typically presented in Section 9 of the Safety Data Sheet or Certificate of Analysis.

nodes.label: productParameterUnit
nodes.description: The unit of measurement for the parameter value presented in Section 9 of the Safety Data Sheet or Certificate of Analysis varies depending on the specific parameter being reported.

nodes.label: productParameterResult
nodes.description: Other types of results, if not numerical values, for parameters presented in Section 9 of the Safety Data Sheet or Certificate of Analysis may include qualitative descriptions like "pass" or "fail," as well as ranges, thresholds, or classifications.

nodes.label: productParameterMethod
nodes.description: Testing methods or standards used for testing parameters.

nodes.label: productParameterRemark
nodes.description: Commentary on the test/parameter presented in the Certificate of Analysis or Safety Data Sheet in Section 9. Selecting "not applicable" is also an option.

nodes.label: parameterClassificationStatement
nodes.description: List of classification statements resulting from the test should include information on whether the result impacts the hazard classification of the product, particularly in the case of properties such as explosives in Safety Data Sheets (SDS).

nodes.label: parameterTestGLPCompliance
nodes.description: Statement indicating whether the test for the parameter was conducted in accordance with the Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) standard, as outlined in ISO/IEC 17025:2017, which specifies general requirements for the competence of testing and calibration laboratories.

nodes.label: parameterDocumentation
nodes.description: Reference to the confirmation of the result typically includes a link to the document containing the confirmation, such as the Certificate of Analysis (CoA).

nodes.label: parameterAfterUseDocumentation
nodes.description: Parameters of a product that may change or become relevant after its intended use are often documented as post-application characteristics. This data can be found in documents such as "Technical Documentation."

nodes.label: classificationRule
nodes.description: Name of the classification rule under which the hazard assessment has been prepared.

nodes.label: classificationHazardInfo
nodes.description: Information if the product is hazardous or not, according to the connected rule.

nodes.label: classificationHazardClassRoute
nodes.description:
Certain classifications must be communicated with the relevant route of exposure according to CLP Regulation (EC) No 1272/2008, which include:
- oral,
- dermal,
- inhalation gas,
- inhalation vapour,
- inhalation dust/mist/fume.

nodes.label: classificationRemark
nodes.description: Additional remarks for classification information supplement the classification details for a particular chemical material. These remarks assist stakeholders in making informed decisions regarding safety, handling, and regulatory compliance.

nodes.label: signalWord
hazardPictogram
nodes.description: A hazard pictogram is a graphical symbol used to convey specific information about the hazards associated with a chemical substance or mixture.

nodes.label: hazardStatement
nodes.description: Describes the nature of a hazard associated with a chemical substance or mixture.

nodes.label: precautionary
nodes.description: A precautionary code for materials is a standardized code used to convey specific precautionary measures that should be taken when handling, storing, or using a chemical substance or mixture.

nodes.label: supplementalRequirements
nodes.description: Supplemental requirements often include additional codes, symbols, or statements beyond those required by regulations.

nodes.label: emergencyTemperatureMinValue
nodes.description: The critical minimum temperature for transporting the product refers to the lowest temperature at which the product can be safely transported without compromising its integrity or functionality. Emergency temperature refers to a temperature that poses a significant risk, potentially leading to hazardous conditions or product damage.

nodes.label: emergencyTemperatureMaxValue
nodes.description: The critical maximum temperature for transporting the product refers to the highest temperature at which the product can be safely transported without degradation or posing a risk. Emergency temperature refers to a temperature that poses a significant risk, potentially leading to hazardous conditions or product damage.

nodes.label: transportOtherInformation
nodes.description: Additional information related to the transport of the product. Relevant details that could aid in making decisions regarding the shipping of the product.

nodes.label: controlTemperature
nodes.description: The controlled temperature for transportation refers to the range of values within which the temperature conditions of goods or products are monitored and regulated during transportation in degree celcius. This information is crucial for mitigating risks associated with temperature-sensitive products, ensuring their integrity and quality throughout the shipping process.

nodes.label: transportPictogram
nodes.description: Links to transport related pictograms.

nodes.label: transportRegulation
nodes.description: Indicate regulations for transportation for example: Land transport (ADR/RID), Inland waterway transport (ADN), Sea transport (IMDG), Air transport (ICAO-TI/IATA-DGR) and so on.

nodes.label: unNumber
nodes.description: The UN number or ID number is a four-digit identification code for substances, mixtures, or articles.

nodes.label: unShippingName
nodes.description: The proper shipping name is provided in column 2, "Name and description," of Table A of Chapter 3.2 Dangerous Goods List of the UN Regulations.

nodes.label: unHazardClassCode
nodes.description: The transport class of the dangerous group according to UN Regulations.

nodes.label: unHazardClassName
nodes.description: The classified dangerous group in transportation refers to the classification of hazardous materials or dangerous goods according to the United Nations (UN) Globally Harmonized System of Classification and Labelling of Chemicals (GHS) and the UN Model Regulations for the Transport of Dangerous Goods.

nodes.label: unPackagingGroup
nodes.description:
Group to which certain substances may be assigned for packaging purposes according to their degree of danger:
Packaging group I: Substances presenting high danger,
Packaging group II: Substances presenting medium danger,
Packaging group III: Substances presenting low danger.
Not all dangerous goods assigned to hazard classes or their divisions will be allocated to Packaging Groups.

nodes.label: unExceptedQuantityCode
nodes.description: Chapter 3.5 of UN Transportation Regulation pertains to dangerous goods packed in excepted quantities, with possible codes including E0, E1, E2, E3, E4, and E5.

nodes.label: environmentallyHazardous
nodes.description: The indication of whether a substance or mixture is environmentally hazardous according to the criteria of the UN Model Regulations (as reflected in ADR, RID, and ADN) and whether it is a marine pollutant according to the IMDG Code and the Emergency Response Procedures for Ships Carrying Dangerous Goods.

nodes.label: unSpecialProvisionsCode
nodes.description: The UN Special Provisions Code refers to a set of additional requirements or conditions specified by the United Nations for the transportation of certain dangerous goods.

nodes.label: unLimitedQuantityValue
nodes.description: Maximum quantity per inner packaging or article for carrying dangerous goods refers to the maximum amount of a hazardous material permitted for transportation.

nodes.label: unLimitedQuantityUnit
nodes.description: Unit of the limited quantity value.

nodes.label: wasteCodeDescription
nodes.description: Description of the related waste code.

nodes.label: wasteCodeRegulation
nodes.description: The name of the regulation that applies to waste codes can vary depending on the region or jurisdiction. Examples include Regulation (EC) No 1013/2006 in the European Union or Annex VIII of the Basel Convention.

nodes.label: wasteCode
nodes.description: A waste code is used to identify the type of waste.

nodes.label: emergencyAvailability
nodes.description: The period of time when emergency phone numbers are available.

nodes.label: emergencyPhoneNumber
nodes.description: The phone number in case of an emergency.

nodes.label: parameterMethodName
nodes.description: The name of the testing method or standard used for testing parameters can vary depending on the context and industry. It might be referred to by a specific code, standard name, or regulation/directive.

nodes.label: parameterMethod
nodes.description: Method conditions and descriptions for the specific method.

nodes.label: hazardPictogramCode
nodes.description: The code of a pictogram refers to a set of symbols used to visually represent and communicate information about the hazards associated with a chemical material.

nodes.label: hazardPictogramImage
nodes.description: Link to the picture of the pictogram. Definition from CLP Regulation (EC) No 1272/2008 : ‘hazard pictogram’ means a graphical composition that includes a symbol plus other graphic elements, such as a border, background pattern or colour that is intended to convey specific information on the hazard concerned;

nodes.label: hazardPictogramName
nodes.description: The meaning or description of a pictogram is the explanation that accompanies it, providing information and context about its purpose and the hazards it represents.

nodes.label: hazardStatementCode
nodes.description: According to CLP Regulation (EC) No 1272/2008, each hazard statement is designated a code, starting with the letter H followed by three digits. These codes can be associated with multiple hazard statements or be assigned to a single statement.

nodes.label: hazardStatementText
nodes.description: Definition from CLP Regulation (EC) No 1272/2008 ‘hazard statement’ means a phrase assigned to a hazard class and category that describes the nature of the hazards of a hazardous substance or mixture, including, where appropriate, the degree of hazard;

nodes.label: precautionaryCode
nodes.description: Each precautionary statement is designated a code, starting with the letter P and followed by three digits.

nodes.label: precautionaryText
nodes.description: Definition from CLP Regulation (EC) No 1272/2008: ‘precautionary statement’ means a phrase that describes recommended measure(s) to minimise or prevent adverse effects resulting from exposure to a hazardous substance or mixture due to its use or disposal.

nodes.label: supplementalRequirementsCode
nodes.description: Supplemental codes used for labeling statements provide additional information or context about the hazards, handling, or regulatory requirements associated with chemical materials. This data can be helpful for stakeholders in managing chemical materials by offering further guidance on safe handling practices, storage conditions, emergency response procedures, or regulatory compliance measures.

nodes.label: supplementalRequirementsText
nodes.description: Any supplemental text used for labeling provides data that could be helpful for stakeholders in managing chemical materials.

nodes.label: supplementalRequirementsPictogram
nodes.description: Supplemental pictures or pictograms used for additional labeling provide data that could be helpful for stakeholders in managing chemical materials.

nodes.label: productLabelingNotes
nodes.description: Labeling notes consist of statements that provide additional information regarding hazardous possible reactions or other important considerations related to the chemical material. These notes help stakeholders understand potential risks and take appropriate precautions when handling, storing, or using the material.

nodes.label: specialProvisionsCode
nodes.description: Code of special provisions or exceptions that must be met.

nodes.label: specialProvisionsInfo
nodes.description: Text aligned with special provisions under UN transport regulation Chapter 3.3.

nodes.label: parameterMethodConditions
nodes.description: The relevant conditions for conducting a measurement or analysis of a chemical parameter ensure the accuracy, reliability, and consistency of the results obtained from the method used. These conditions are typically presented in the Certificate of Analysis or Safety Data Sheet, specifically in Section 9.

nodes.label: parameterMethodDescription
nodes.description: Short description of the method used to determine parameter presented in Certificate of Analysis or Safety Data Sheet in Section 9.

nodes.label: SpecificCharacteristic
nodes.description: The Characteristic to describe product specific parameters of the electric drive.

nodes.label: UnspecificCharacteristic
nodes.description: Characteristic for the product unspecific parameters.

nodes.label: ParameterCharacteristic
nodes.description: Parameter Characteristic for the chemical material.

nodes.label: HazardAssessmentCharacteristic
nodes.description: Characteristic for the hazard assessment.

nodes.label: TransportCharacteristic
nodes.description: Characteristic for transport.

nodes.label: DisposalCharacteristic
nodes.description: Characteristic for disposal related attributes.

nodes.label: SafetyCharacteristic
nodes.description: Characteristic for safety information.

nodes.label: ComplianceCharacteristic
nodes.description: Characteristic for compliance attributes.

nodes.label: ParameterAfterUseCharacteristic
nodes.description: Characteristic for parameters of the chemical material after use.

nodes.label: LabelingCharacteristic
nodes.description: Characteristic for material labeling.

nodes.label: ProductTransportCharacteristic
nodes.description: Product transportation Characteristic.

nodes.label: WasteCodeCharacteristic
nodes.description: Characteristic for the waste code of the material.

nodes.label: EmergencyList
nodes.description: List of information on how to proceed in case of an emergency.

nodes.label: PrecautionaryList
nodes.description: List for precautionary codes.

nodes.label: LinkCharacteristic
nodes.description: Characteristic for a simple web link.

nodes.label: IntegerCharacteristic
nodes.description: Characteristic for integer values.

nodes.label: SpecificEntity
nodes.description: The Entity for product specific parameters of the electric drive.

nodes.label: UnspecificEntity
nodes.description: Entity for the product unspecific parameters.

nodes.label: ParameterEntity
nodes.description: Parameter Entity for the chemical material.

nodes.label: HazardAssessmentEntity
nodes.description: Entity for the hazard assessment.

nodes.label: TransportEntity
nodes.description: Entity for transport attributes.

nodes.label: DisposalEntity
nodes.description: Entity for disposal related attributes.

nodes.label: SafetyEntity
nodes.description: Entity for safety information.

nodes.label: ComplianceEntity
nodes.description: Entity for compliance attributes.

nodes.label: ParameterBeforeUseEntity
nodes.description: Entity for parameters of the chemical material before use.

nodes.label: ParameterAfterUseEntity
nodes.description: Characteristic for parameters of the chemical material after use.

nodes.label: ClassificationEntity
nodes.description: Entity for the hazard classification.

nodes.label: LabelingEntity
nodes.description: Entity for the labeling of the chemical material.

nodes.label: ProductTransportEntity
nodes.description: Entity for transport.

nodes.label: UNTransportEntity
nodes.description: Entity with transport related attributes.

nodes.label: WasteCodeEntity
nodes.description: Entity for the waste code of the material.

nodes.label: EmergencyEntity
nodes.description: Entity for information on how to proceed in case of an emergency.

nodes.label: ParameterMethodEntity
nodes.description: Testing methods or standards used for testing parameters with method names.

nodes.label: HazardPictogramEntity
nodes.description: Entity for the hazard pictogram.

nodes.label: HazardStatementEntity
nodes.description: Entity for the hazard statement.

nodes.label: PrecautionaryEntity
nodes.description: Entity for precautionary codes.

nodes.label: SupplementalRequirementsEntity
nodes.description: Entity for supplemental requirements.

nodes.label: SpecialProvisionsCodeEntity
nodes.description: Entity for the special provisions code.

nodes.label: ParameterMethodsEntity
nodes.description: Entity for method conditions and descriptions.

nodes.label: MaterialEnumeration
nodes.description: Enumeration with the values substance, mixture and article.

nodes.label: RouteEnumeration
nodes.description: Enumeration with oral, dermal, inhalation gas, inhalation vapour, inhalation dust/mist/fume and not applicable

nodes.label: SignalWordEnumeration
nodes.description: Enumeration with Danger and Warning.

nodes.label: PackagingGroupEnumeration
nodes.description: Enumeration to match "I", "II", "III", or "not applicable".

nodes.label: QuantityCodeEnumeration
nodes.description: Enumeration for the codes E0, E1, E2, E3, E4, and E5.

nodes.label: ParameterBeforeUseList
nodes.description: List of parameters of the chemical material before use.

nodes.label: ClassificationList
nodes.description: List for the hazard classification.

nodes.label: UNTransportList
nodes.description: List of transport related attributes from the UN.

nodes.label: StringList
nodes.description: List of text strings.

nodes.label: ParameterMethodList
nodes.description: List of testing methods or standards used for testing parameters.

nodes.label: HazardPictogramList
nodes.description: List of hazard pictograms.

nodes.label: HazardStatementList
nodes.description: List for the hazard statement.

nodes.label: SupplementalRequirementsList
nodes.description: List for supplemental requirements.

nodes.label: SpecialProvisionsCodeList
nodes.description: List of the special provisions codes.

nodes.label: ParameterMethodsList
nodes.description: List of conditions and descriptions for the specific method.

nodes.label: ProductComplianceCountryTrait
nodes.description: Trait restricting the input to valid country codes.

nodes.label: UNNumberTrait
nodes.description: Trait for the UN number.

nodes.label: PrecautionaryCodeTrait
nodes.description: Trait for the precautionary codes.

nodes.label: CountryCodeRegEx
nodes.description: Regex pattern including all valid ISO 3166 country codes, ensuring a precise match.

nodes.label: UNNumberConstraint
nodes.description: Constraint to four digits numbers.

nodes.label: PrecautionaryCodeConstraint
nodes.description: Constraint starting with the letter P followed by three digits.

nodes.label: CelsiusDegreeMeasurement
nodes.description: Measurement as float in celsius degree.

nodes.label: FloatQuantityValue
nodes.description: Value as float.
